steps.title: Identify the coefficients in standard form
steps.content: The equation $x^2 + 49x + 1 = 0$ is in standard form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$. Reading the coefficients directly: $a = 1$, $b = 49$, $c = 1$.

steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula and match to the correct choice
steps.content: Substituting $a = 1$, $b = 49$, $c = 1$ into the quadratic formula: $$x = \frac{-49 \pm \sqrt{(49)^2 - 4(1)(1)}}{2(1)}$$ This gives two solutions using the $\pm$ sign. One solution (using the plus sign) is $$x = \frac{-49 + \sqrt{(49)^2 - 4(1)(1)}}{2(1)}$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
